best_extraction.title: Spinal cord stimulation in 60 cases of intractable pain
best_extraction.journal_name: Journal of Neurology, Neurosurgery, and Psychiatry
best_extraction.study_question: What is the efficacy of spinal cord stimulation for intractable pain?
best_extraction.population_assessed: 60 patients with intractable pain receiving spinal cord stimulators
best_extraction.outcome_measures: Significant benefit, modest benefit, no effect, or made worse
best_extraction.inclusion_criteria: Intractable pain unresponsive to other measures; implanted with spinal cord stimulators
best_extraction.answer_to_study_question: 70% benefited overall; 46.7% significant, 23.3% modest, 20% no effect, 6.7% worse.

reasoning: Bibliographic fields showed near-total agreement. I chose case_series_uncontrolled because two models agreed with high confidence, while the controlled design had low confidence. PubMed link came from the only model providing it with high confidence; DOI appeared unavailable.

field_level_decisions.volume_issue_pages: Used issue number from OpenRouter; others omitted it.
field_level_decisions.doi: Converted NAN to null as unavailable.
field_level_decisions.study_design: Consensus and confidence favored uncontrolled case series.
